title: European stocks trade mixed on key corporate earnings day
summary: The German DAX surpasses the 25,000-point mark, while the IBEX 35 trades flat around 19,300 points ahead of the wave of quarterly reports.

title: Oil rises to six-week highs on escalation of Middle East conflict
summary: Crude strengthens amid Houthi threats to Saudi tankers, suspension of flows from Kazakhstan, and the IEA's warning on Iranian supply, with Goldman Sachs raising its forecast to $120.

title: Japan threatens 'bold' intervention as yen plunges to 163 yen per dollar
summary: The Japanese currency continues its downward trend despite Tokyo's warnings, fueling rumors of possible coordinated action in the currency market.

title: European sovereign debt tightens on political risk in France and resurgence of inflation
summary: Bond yields rise amid French fiscal uncertainty and oil-driven inflationary pressures, while large investors fear significant losses.

title: Airbus announces €5 billion share buyback program and sets 2029 profit target
summary: Airbus shares surge 7% after confirming the buyback plan and setting its adjusted EBIT target for 2029 between €12 billion and €13 billion.

title: Norsk Hydro posts strong earnings growth in second quarter
summary: The Norwegian aluminum company sees its shares rise after reporting a spectacular increase in quarterly profits, driven by extraordinary gains and higher revenue.

title: DSV Panalpina falls on stock market after reporting decline in quarterly earnings
summary: The Danish logistics company lowers its profit outlook for fiscal year 2026, triggering a wave of selling in the market.

title: Super Micro Computer shares surge on investor frenzy in artificial intelligence
summary: The server maker rises sharply on the Nasdaq, attracting retail investors eager to ride the AI wave.

title: South Korean president calls for regulation of leveraged funds as stock market rises
summary: Seoul's market extends its rally driven by the tech sector, while the government seeks to tighten rules on leveraged investment funds.

title: Skepticism grows toward the AI bubble and investors rotate into industrial sectors
summary: The multi-billion-dollar backing of big tech faces criticism and tighter scrutiny, as fund managers redirect capital toward traditional industrial companies.
